methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 balances held by Genesis contracts (virtualTokenAddress deposits; agentTokenAddress balances for distribution; any other ERC20 swept via withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized)
ERC20 balances held by AgentTax (taxToken collected; assetToken post-swap before distribution; any token withdrawable by ADMIN_ROLE)
ERC20 balances held by BondingTax (taxToken collected by bonding router; swapped into assetToken and sent to treasury; any token withdrawable by ADMIN_ROLE)
ERC20 balances held by FRouter/FPair system (pair reserves tokenA/tokenB; taxVault collects buy/sell fees; graduate() extracts assetToken from pair to executor)
ERC20 balances held by TBABonus (assetToken used to pay bonuses)
ERC20 balances held by LPRefund (taxToken inventory used to pay refunds)
ERC20 balances held by TokenSaver (any ERC20 held can be moved by TOKEN_SAVER_ROLE)
ERC20 balances held by Airdrop contract (temporarily receives _total from caller then distributes; can hold dust)

Protocol-minted ERC20s: VirtualToken (VIRTUAL) supply/mint authority; veVirtualToken (veVIRTUAL) voting power supply controlled by owner via oracleTransfer; FERC20 launched tokens minted at deploy and later burned via burnFrom
NFT assets: ContributionNft ERC721; ServiceNft ERC721; (external) AgentNft ERC721 and Token Bound Accounts (TBAs) referenced as recipients of funds
state_assets: Genesis: mapAddrToVirtuals (user committed virtuals), claimableAgentTokens (distribution ledger), participants array, refundUserCountForFailed, startTime/endTime, reserveAmount/maxContributionVirtualAmount, agentTokenAddress, isFailed/isCancelled, genesis metadata (name/ticker/cores), DAO/TBA params
FGenesis: global params (fee, duration, reserve, thresholds, supplies, addresses), genesisContracts mapping (id->Genesis address), genesisID counter, roles

Bonding: tokenInfo mapping (creator, pair, agentToken, trading flags, metadata), tokenInfos array, profile mapping (creator->tokens), fee settings, gradThreshold/maxTx/assetRate/initialSupply, deploy params for agentFactory graduation
FRouter: factory address, assetToken, taxManager, roles; critical routing math and fee handling paths
FFactory: pair mapping, pairs array, router address, taxVault, buyTax/sellTax, roles
FPair: pool reserves/k/lastUpdated, router binding, tokenA/tokenB addresses

AgentTax: taxHistory (txhash replay protection), agentTaxAmounts (collected/swapped accounting), swap params (router/asset/feeRate/creatorFeeRate/thresholds/treasury), cached recipients per agentId, tbaBonus address
BondingTax: swap params (router/bondingRouter/assetToken), thresholds, slippage, treasury
TBABonus: bonusRate, per-agent allowance and paid tracking
AgentDAO: proposal maturity accumulators, validator score checkpoints, totalScore, agentNft pointer
VirtualGenesisDAO: early execution flags, quorum checkpoints, EXECUTOR_ROLE
ContributionNft: contribution graph (parent/children), virtualId per tokenId, core per tokenId, modelContributions/modelDatasets, admin and eloCalculator pointers
ServiceNft: core/maturity/impact per serviceId, coreServices/coreDatasets indices, datasetImpactWeight, personaNft/contributionNft addresses
AgentInference: inferenceCount per agentId; token and agentNft addresses
privilege_assets: AccessControl roles across upgradeable contracts: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (Genesis, FGenesis, AgentTax, BondingTax, FRouter, FFactory, TBABonus, LPRefund, VirtualGenesisDAO), plus custom roles (FACTORY_ROLE, ADMIN_ROLE, OPERATION_ROLE, EXECUTOR_ROLE, CREATOR_ROLE, TOKEN_SAVER_ROLE)

Ownable authorities: Bonding.owner (can change economic params + deploy params), EloCalculator.owner (setK), ServiceNft.owner (setDatasetImpactWeight), FERC20.owner (burnFrom, maxTx controls, exclusions), VirtualToken.owner (mint), veVirtualToken.owner (oracleTransfer)
Genesis withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized callable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (granted to factory address at initialization)
FRouter EXECUTOR_ROLE gating all trade/liq/graduate/approval flows; ADMIN_ROLE can set taxManager
FFactory ADMIN_ROLE can set router/tax params; CREATOR_ROLE can createPair
AgentTax ADMIN_ROLE can change router/asset/fees/thresholds/treasury/withdraw; EXECUTOR_ROLE can record taxes and force swaps via handleAgentTaxes and dcaSell
BondingTax ADMIN_ROLE can change router/bondingRouter/asset/slippage/thresholds/treasury/withdraw; bondingRouter address has exclusive swapForAsset ability
TBABonus ADMIN_ROLE sets rates/allowances; EXECUTOR_ROLE required for actual payouts (distributeBonus)
LPRefund EXECUTOR_ROLE can pay refunds; ADMIN_ROLE can manualRefund and withdraw
reputation_assets: DAO governance outcomes and legitimacy (AgentDAO, VirtualGenesisDAO, VirtualProtocolDAO): proposal execution rights, quorum settings, early execution behavior
Voting power integrity in veVirtualToken / IVotes-based governors (oracleTransfer-controlled balances affect governance)
Validator scoring and maturity/ELO outputs in AgentDAO impacting ServiceNft scoring and protocol credibility
Contribution and Service NFT records (accepted proposals mirrored into NFTs; impacts downstream rewards/recognition)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: Genesis: ReentrancyGuard + AccessControlUpgradeable (upgradeable-style initializer but contract is deployed with `new Genesis()` and then initialized). Bonding: Initializable + ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable + OwnableUpgradeable (constructor disables initializers). AgentTax/BondingTax/TBABonus/LPRefund/FGenesis/FRouter/FFactory: Initializable + AccessControlUpgradeable (+ ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable where noted). ServiceNft/ContributionNft: Initializable + ERC721Upgradeable + Enumerable + URIStorage (+ OwnableUpgradeable for ServiceNft). AgentDAO: Governor*Upgradeable stack (Settings, CountingSimpleUpgradeable, Storage, Votes, QuorumFraction). VirtualGenesisDAO/VirtualProtocolDAO: non-upgradeable OZ Governor stack; VirtualGenesisDAO additionally AccessControl. FERC20: non-upgradeable custom ERC20 + Ownable. veVirtualToken: non-upgradeable ERC20Votes/Permit + Ownable with transfers disabled and owner-only oracleTransfer. VirtualToken: ERC20Capped + Ownable. TokenSaver: AccessControl (non-upgradeable). Airdrop: standalone assembly-based distributor. FPair: standalone pair contract with router-only control.
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin: AccessControlUpgradeable, OwnableUpgradeable, Initializable, ReentrancyGuard/ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable, SafeERC20, ERC721Upgradeable (+Enumerable, +URIStorage), Governor modules (upgradeable and non-upgradeable), ERC20Votes/ERC20Permit/ERC20Capped

External protocol-style interfaces: IAgentFactoryV3 (agent creation/execution), IAgentNft (TBA/DAO registry), IGovernor/IVotes (governance), IRouter (swap router abstraction), Aerodrome router via IAeroRouter (through AeroAdaptor)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No Diamond proxy pattern present. Multiple contracts are upgradeable-implementation style (Initializable + _disableInitializers in constructor) suggesting deployment behind proxies (UUPS or Transparent not shown in codebase). Some contracts are upgradeable-style but directly instantiated via `new` (Genesis via GenesisLib) which is an architectural inconsistency but not a proxy upgrade pattern. No explicit proxy admin/upgrade functions are present in the analyzed files.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Role-based (AccessControl/AccessControlUpgradeable) for factory/router/tax/refund/bonus modules; Ownable for Bonding, ServiceNft, EloCalculator, token contracts. Governor-based access (`onlyGovernance`) for quorum updates in VirtualGenesisDAO; AccessControl EXECUTOR_ROLE for earlyExecute. Notable: FRouter critical functions are gated by EXECUTOR_ROLE, but initialization grants only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to msg.sender; role assignment for ADMIN_ROLE/EXECUTOR_ROLE is external to this code. FGenesis createGenesis is public (no role), but charges fee via ERC20 transferFrom.

state_changes: refundUserCountForFailed += 1 (per refunded participant index where virtualsAmt>0)

external_calls: FERC20(srcTokenAddress).burnFrom(account, balance) (requires token owner privileges; likely to revert unless Bonding owns token)

state_changes: taxToken allowance moved from oldRouter to new router

external_calls: _token.transfer(recipient, amount) in loop via assembly call

state_changes: ERC20 balances/voting checkpoints via _update (mint/burn/transfer semantics depending on from/to)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Upgradeable base (AccessControlUpgradeable) but deployed as a standalone contract via `new Genesis()` in GenesisLib; no proxy upgrade surface, but initializer exposure exists until called.
Initializable implementation style; proxy not shown. initialize() is external and must be called once on deployed instance.
Implementation-style with disabled initializers in constructor; intended for proxy but proxy not included in codebase.
Implementation-style; grants ADMIN roles in initialize.
Implementation-style; bondingRouter is a privileged caller for swapForAsset.
Critical router is role-gated; only DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE granted in initialize; ADMIN_ROLE/EXECUTOR_ROLE provisioning must occur elsewhere.
Factory is role-gated; router must be set before pair creation.
Multiple inheritance of ERC721Upgradeable/Enumerable/URIStorage has known storage layout sensitivity across upgrades; ensure OZ upgrade-safe ordering if proxied.
Multiple inheritance of ERC721Upgradeable/Enumerable/URIStorage; upgrade storage layout must be handled carefully if proxied.
GovernorUpgradeable diamond inheritance is complex; upgrades require strict OZ-guided storage layout discipline.
Implementation-style; distributeBonus is public but gated by EXECUTOR_ROLE (returns early if not authorized).
